description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block wall segment composed of two horizontal rows of large, roughly rectangular stone blocks. The stones have a natural, slightly irregular texture and coloration, with visible seams and mortar lines between each block. The surface is predominantly a muted tan and gray, with subtle variations in shading that mimic natural stone. The block is relatively thin in depth compared to its length and height, making it suitable as a low barrier or edging. The top and front faces are flat, while the sides reveal the narrow profile of the structure. There are no visible engravings, markings, or decorative elements, giving it a utilitarian appearance.
description: A rectangular, tan-gray stone block wall segment with two rows of large, natural-looking stones and visible mortar lines. The surface is textured and utilitarian, suitable as a low barrier or edging.
description_view_0: Front view showing the two horizontal rows of rectangular stone blocks with visible seams and a flat top.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the block, emphasizing its narrow depth compared to its length and height.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the same two rows of stone blocks, similar to the front but with less detail on the seams.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and the layered stone construction.
attribute_location_context: urban retaining wall or garden landscaping
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean with natural stone texture and minor color variation.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: forms part of a continuous wall
